betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Particle Chamber Outputs: Beam Blocks & Beam Ports§f

§aBeam blocks and beam ports are the IO interface for every particle chamber multiblock.§f
The §bParticle Chamber Beam Block§f (`qmd:particle_chamber_beam`) and §bParticle Chamber Beam Port§f (`qmd:particle_chamber_beam_port`) form the spine of every Target Chamber, Beam Dump, Decay Chamber, and Collision Chamber — they carry the particle beam from a port on the outer face inward to the chamber's central target tile.

§6Particle Chamber Beam Block Recipe§f:
Per the QMD crafting recipe handler, Particle Chamber Beam Blocks are crafted in a vanilla §bminecraft.crafting§f grid from:
- §bStainless Steel Ingot§f (`qmd:ingot_alloy:2`)
- §bTungsten Ingot§f (`hbm:ingot_tungsten` / NC equivalent)
- §bBeamline Pipe§f (`qmd:beamline`)

§6Beam Block Role in Multiblocks§f:
- The geometric center of every chamber is a §bParticle Chamber§f (target) tile; the multiblock validation requires beam blocks to run from each outer-face beam port all the way to the center.
- §bChamber Beam Ports§f are IO interfaces: they can be set to §bINPUT§f, §bOUTPUT§f, or §bDISABLED§f via the §bBeam Meter§f tool (Shift + right-click).
- The §bAccelerator Beam Port§f variant is used on §bLINAC§f, §bSynchrotron§f, §bBeam Diverter§f, §bBeam Splitter§f, and §bDecelerator§f multiblocks.

§6Chamber Port Mode Rules§f:
- §bTarget Chamber§f: exactly 1 INPUT port required (`TargetChamberLogic.java:126`).
- §bBeam Dump§f: ALL ports must be INPUT — output is fluid only (`BeamDumpLogic.java:129`).
- §bCollision Chamber§f: typically 1 input on each of two opposing faces.
- §bDecay Chamber§f: 1 input, 1 or more outputs.

§eTip§f: Always place §bBeam Meter§f-readable ports on the outside of a chamber for easy diagnostics. The beam port is the only point where you can confirm a particle species, focus, and energy without breaking the multiblock.
